rag_rules: Place input range assumptions in component type annexes using visible feature names and concrete bounds.
Use assign statements only for implementation-level output bindings with existing features or declared variables.
Generate syntactically valid AGREE contracts within annex agree blocks using proper scope rules.
generic_rules: Ensure all AGREE expressions reference visible identifiers from the AADL model.
Validate that assumption and guarantee types match the actual data types of referenced features.
Avoid encoding AADL properties as AGREE variables unless explicitly exposed as typed ports or constants.
Use boolean helper declarations for range comparisons with proper type alignment.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Component type contracts should use feature names directly; implementation contracts may use subcomponent ports only when valid.
Equation declarations and guarantees must use valid AGREE syntax with correct boolean expressions.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level guarantees in component type scopes.
Do not reference AADL property names like Period or Deadline as AGREE variables.
Do not convert Boolean domain values into integer comparisons unless the port is explicitly typed as bool.
